basic_details.job_title: Central Sanskrit University (CSU) Non-Teaching Recruitment 2026 Notification for 144 Posts - Apply Online through SAMARTH Portal
basic_details.post_name: Registrar, Assistant Registrar, System Analyst, Section Officer, Nursing Officer, Assistant, Guest House Manager, Junior Engineer, Personal Assistant, Professional Assistant, Technical Assistant (Shikshashastra/Education Lab.), Technical Assistant (Computer Lab.), Stenographer, Upper Division Clerk, Library Assistant, Lower Division Clerk, Driver, Multi-Tasking Staff, Library Attendant, Medical Attendant/Dresser
basic_details.short_information: Central Sanskrit University (CSU) has published the Non-Teaching Posts Notification 2026 on 16 March 2026 at sanskrit.nic.in. The recruitment drive aims to fill 144 Non-Teaching Posts positions, including roles such as Registrar, Assistant Registrar, System Analyst, and more. Candidates within the age range of 32 to 57 years (as of 16 April 2026) and holding qualifications such as 10th, 12th, ITI, Bachelor's, Master's, B.E./B.Tech., M.E./M.Tech. can apply online from 16 March 2026 to 20 May 2026. Find all details about the CSU Non-Teaching Posts new vacancy 2026, including eligibility, online

application_fee.details: Fee payment mode: Online only through the SAMARTH portal link: https://sanskritnt.samarth.edu.in/ Fee once paid is non-refundable under any circumstances. Separate fee is required for each post applied for. Applications submitted without requisite fee shall be rejected. Any other mode of payment except online shall not be accepted.

age_limit.age_details: 32 to 40 Years (as on 16.04.2026); Registrar: Preferably below 57 years
age_limit.details: Registrar: Preferably below 57 years. Assistant Registrar & System Analyst: Not exceeding 40 years. Section Officer, Nursing Officer, Assistant, Guest House Manager, Junior Engineer, Personal Assistant & Professional Assistant: Not exceeding 35 years. Technical Assistant (Shikshashastra & Computer Lab.), Stenographer, UDC, Library Assistant, LDC, Driver, MTS, Library Attendant & Medical Attendant/Dresser: Not exceeding 32 years. Age relaxation as per Government of India rules for SC/ST/OBC/PwBD/Ex-Servicemen and other eligible reserved categories. Regular employees of Central/State Govt./Central Universities/UGC maintained Institutions/PSUs/Autonomous Bodies: Age relaxation as per Govt. of India rules. Ex-Servicemen: Upper age limit for Group 'C' posts is relaxable up to 40 years (General) and 45 years (SC/ST) for candidates who have rendered 3 years continuous regular service. Deputation candidates for posts below Pay Level-14: Must be less than 56 years of age; for Pay Level-14 and above: less than 58 years of age.

qualification.education_qualification: 10th Pass / 10+2 / ITI / Diploma / Bachelor's Degree / Master's Degree / B.E./B.Tech. / M.E./M.Tech. – as per post-wise eligibility
qualification.details: Registrar (Level-14): Master's Degree with at least 55% marks or equivalent grade. At least 15 years of experience as Assistant Professor in Academic Level-11 and above, OR with 8 years in Academic Level-12 and above including as Associate Professor with experience in educational administration, OR comparable experience in research establishments/higher education institutions, OR 15 years of administrative experience of which 8 years as Deputy Registrar or equivalent post. Direct/Deputation for a tenure of five years or till attaining age of superannuation (62 years), whichever is earlier. Deputation: Holding analogous post or eight years' experience at Pay Level-12. Assistant Registrar (Level-10): Master's Degree with at least 55% marks or equivalent grade. Appointment through All India open competition by written test and interview. Deputation: Officers holding analogous posts on regular basis or with 5 years regular service in Level 7/Level 8 in Central/State Government, Universities or Autonomous Organizations; Knowledge of Computer Applications. System Analyst (Level-10): B.E./B.Tech. in Computer Science & Engineering/Electronics Engineering with 5 years programming experience in C/C++/JAVA, databases (MySQL/ORACLE/PHP), under WINDOWS/LINUX/UNIX platforms. OR M.E./M.Tech. in Computer Science & Engineering/Electronics Engineering / M.Sc. Computer Science / MCA with 3 years programming experience as above. Section Officer (Level-7): Bachelor's Degree in any discipline from a recognised University/Institute. 3 years' experience as Assistant in Level-6 OR 8 years as UDC in Level-4 in Central/State Govt./University/PSU/Autonomous Institutions or equivalent in reputed private companies/banks with annual turnover of at least Rs. 200 Crores. Proficiency in Computer Operation, noting and drafting. Deputation: Officers holding analogous post on regular basis or with 3 years regular service in Level-6 or equivalent. Nursing Officer (Level-7): B.Sc. (Hons.) in Nursing / Regular B.Sc. Nursing / Post Basic B.Sc. Nursing from a recognised University or Institute; Registered as a Nurse or Nurse and Midwife (RN or RN and RM) with State Nursing Council; 6 months' experience in minimum 50-bedded hospital after acquiring the qualification. OR Diploma in General Nursing Midwifery from recognised Board or Council; Registered as RN or RN and RM with State Nursing Council; 2.5 years' experience in minimum 50-bedded hospital. Assistant (Level-6): Bachelor's Degree from a recognised University/Institution. 3 years' experience as UDC or equivalent in Level-4 in Central/State Govt./University/PSU/Autonomous Bodies or equivalent in reputed private companies/corporate banks with minimum annual turnover of Rs. 200 Crores. Proficiency in Typing, Computer Applications, noting and drafting. Guest House Manager (Level-6): Bachelor's Degree in Hotel Management or allied field with 1 year relevant experience; OR Diploma in Hotel Management or allied field with 2 years relevant experience. Knowledge of Computer Applications. Junior Engineer (Level-6): Bachelor's Degree in Engineering/Technology in relevant field with 1 year relevant experience; OR Diploma in Engineering in relevant field with 3 years' experience in CPWD/State PWD/Similar Organised Services/Statutory or Autonomous Organizations/Universities/reputed private construction companies with annual turnover of at least Rs. 200 Crores. Personal Assistant (Level-6): Bachelor's Degree in any discipline from a recognised University/Institute. Proficiency in Stenography in English or Hindi at minimum 100 wpm. Proficiency in Typing in English @ 35 wpm or Hindi @ 30 wpm. Knowledge of Computer Applications. 2 years' experience as Stenographer or equivalent in Central/State Govt./University/Research Institution/Central/State Autonomous Institution/reputed private institutions with turnover of Rs. 200 Crores. Desirable: Proficiency in English and good communication skills. Skill Test: Dictation 10 minutes @ 100 wpm; Transcription: 40 minutes (English)/55 minutes (Hindi). Professional Assistant (Level-6): Master's Degree in Library & Information Science from recognised University/Institution with 2 years' experience in relevant field in University/Research Establishment/Central/State Govt./PSU/Autonomous Institutions. OR Bachelor's Degree in Library/Library and Information Science with 3 years' experience in relevant field. Knowledge of Computer Applications. Technical Assistant – Shikshashastra/Education Lab. (Level-5): Bachelor's Degree with minimum 3 years of working and maintenance/operation experience of Scientific Instruments in the Laboratory in University/Research Establishment/Central/State Govt./PSU/Autonomous Bodies or Private Organization with annual turnover of at least Rs. 200 Crores. Technical Assistant – Computer Lab. (Level-5): Bachelor's Degree in Engineering/Technology in Computer Science and Technology/Information Technology from any recognised University/Institute; OR Master's Degree in Computer Science/Technology/MCA. At least 2 years' experience in relevant field in University/Research Establishment/Central/State Govt./PSU/Autonomous Bodies or Private Organization with annual turnover of at least Rs. 200 Crores. Stenographer (Level-4): Bachelor's Degree in any discipline from a recognised University/Institute. Proficiency in Stenography in English or Hindi at minimum 80 wpm. Proficiency in Typing in English @ 35 wpm or Hindi @ 30 wpm. Knowledge of Computer Applications. Skill Test: Dictation 10 minutes @ 80 wpm; Transcription: 50 minutes (English)/65 minutes (Hindi). Desirable: Proficiency in English and good communication skills. Upper Division Clerk – UDC (Level-4): Bachelor's Degree from any recognised University/Institute. 2 years' experience as LDC/equivalent posts in University/Research Establishment/Central/State Govt./PSU/Autonomous Bodies or equivalent in reputed private companies/corporate banks with minimum annual turnover of Rs. 200 Crores. Speed in English Typing @ 35 wpm OR Hindi Typing @ 30 wpm. Proficiency in Computer Operations. Library Assistant (Level-4): Bachelor's Degree in Library & Information Science or equivalent from a recognised University. Typing speed of 30 words per minute in English. Knowledge of Computer Applications. Lower Division Clerk – LDC (Level-2): Bachelor's Degree from any recognised University/Institute. English Typing @ 35 wpm OR Hindi Typing @ 30 wpm (35 wpm and 30 wpm correspond to 10500 KDPH/9000 KDPH on an average of 5 key depressions for each word). Proficiency in Computer Operations. Driver (Level-2): 10th Pass from any recognised Board. Valid Commercial Driving License for Light/Medium/Heavy Vehicles issued by Competent Authority with no adverse endorsement. Knowledge of motor mechanism (ability to remove minor defects in vehicles). At least 5 years' driving experience in an organization. Multi-Tasking Staff – MTS (Level-1): 10th Pass from a recognised Board; OR ITI Pass. Library Attendant (Level-1): 10+2 or equivalent from a recognised Board. Certificate Course in Library Science from a recognised Institution. 1 year experience in a University/College/Educational Institution Library. Basic knowledge of Computer Applications. Medical Attendant / Dresser (Level-1): 10+2 or equivalent with Science from a recognised Board. 1 year experience as Attendant in related field in any Government/recognised hospitals/diagnostics centres/accredited centres. Knowledge of Computer.

vacancy_details.PwBD Reserved (Horizontal): Distributed across posts (Categories a to e)

salary_details.details: Registrar: Pay Level-14 – Rs. 1,44,200 to Rs. 2,18,200 (as per 7th CPC) Assistant Registrar & System Analyst: Pay Level-10 – Rs. 56,100 to Rs. 1,77,500 Section Officer & Nursing Officer: Pay Level-07 – Rs. 44,900 to Rs. 1,42,400 Assistant, Guest House Manager, Junior Engineer, Personal Assistant & Professional Assistant: Pay Level-06 – Rs. 35,400 to Rs. 1,12,400 Technical Assistant (Shikshashastra/Education Lab. & Computer Lab.): Pay Level-05 – Rs. 29,200 to Rs. 92,300 Stenographer, Upper Division Clerk & Library Assistant: Pay Level-04 – Rs. 25,500 to Rs. 81,100 Lower Division Clerk & Driver: Pay Level-02 – Rs. 19,900 to Rs. 63,200 Multi-Tasking Staff, Library Attendant & Medical Attendant/Dresser: Pay Level-01 – Rs. 18,000 to Rs. 56,900

selection_process: Most posts will be filled through Direct Recruitment; certain posts through Deputation or Direct Recruitment failing which by Deputation. Eligible candidates will undergo a Written Test (OMR Based / Descriptive) in the first instance. Candidates shortlisted through Written Test will be called for Skill Test and/or Interview (as applicable to the post). Examination Centres: Delhi
Kolkata & Bengaluru (candidate may choose one city). Examination Schedules (to avoid conflicts): Schedule 1: System Analyst

Lower Division Clerk. Candidates applying for multiple posts within the same Schedule cannot appear for both simultaneously. For Registrar post: Direct/Deputation for tenure of 5 years or till superannuation (62 years)
whichever is earlier. Detailed Scheme of Examination and Syllabus available at: https://www.sanskrit.nic.in/scheme_of_exam.php
how_to_apply: Visit the official CSU website: www.sanskrit.nic.in under the "Recruitment/Notification" section.
Register and apply online through the SAMARTH Link: https://sanskritnt.samarth.edu.in/
Upload clear, high-quality scanned copies of all requisite documents during online application.
Pay the prescribed application fee online only through the payment link in the application portal.
After successful online submission, print a signed hard copy of the application form.

Send the signed hard copy along with self-attested copies of all supporting documents through Registered Post within 15 days from the last date of online application (04/06/2026) to the appropriate address: For Registrar Post: The Vice Chancellor, Central Sanskrit University, 56-57, Institutional Area, Janakpuri, New Delhi-110058. For all other posts: The Registrar, Central Sanskrit University, 56-57, Institutional Area, Janakpuri, New Delhi-110058.
For Registrar Post: The Vice Chancellor, Central Sanskrit University, 56-57, Institutional Area, Janakpuri, New Delhi-110058.
For all other posts: The Registrar, Central Sanskrit University, 56-57, Institutional Area, Janakpuri, New Delhi-110058.
The last date for online application is 20 May 2026 by 11:30 PM (IST).
Candidates must complete both steps (online submission + hard copy submission) within the prescribed timelines; failure to comply with either may lead to rejection.
important_instructions: Applications must be submitted Online through the SAMARTH Link: https://sanskritnt.samarth.edu.in/ available on the University's website www.sanskrit.nic.in under "Recruitment/Notification". After online submission, candidates must send a duly signed hard copy of the application along with self-attested copies of all supporting documents through Registered Post within 15 days from the last date of online application. Hard copy for Registrar post to be sent to: The Vice Chancellor, Central Sanskrit University, 56-57, Institutional Area, Janakpuri, New Delhi-110058. Hard copy for all other posts to be sent to: The Registrar, Central Sanskrit University, 56-57, Institutional Area, Janakpuri, New Delhi-110058. Mere possession of eligibility conditions does not entitle a candidate to be called for written/skill tests. Candidates must apply separately for each post with separate requisite fee. The University posts belong to All-India Cadre — once appointed, transfer requests will not be considered for a minimum lock-in period of 10 years. Candidates currently in service (State/Central Govt./PSU/Autonomous Bodies) must submit a No Objection Certificate (NOC) from the employer at the time of document verification/interview. PwBD candidates with less than 40% benchmark disability must submit a valid Disability Certificate uploaded with the application. OBC NCL certificate must be issued on or after 01.04.2025 to be considered valid for this advertisement. EWS certificate (current financial year) must be valid on or before the last date of online application submission. Candidates are advised to apply well before the last date to avoid technical issues. All future updates/notices will be published only on www.sanskrit.nic.in — candidates are advised to visit the website regularly. Canvassing in any form will result in disqualification. For technical support during online application: [email protected] In case of any dispute, jurisdiction shall be Hon'ble Courts in Delhi.

faq.question: Q1. How many total vacancies are announced in CSU Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: A: Total 144 Non-Teaching vacancies across 20 posts.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply online for Central Sanskrit University posts?
faq.answer: A: Last date for online application is 20 May 2026

faq.question: Q3. What is the pay scale for Registrar in Central Sanskrit University?
Q4. Is hard copy of application mandatory for CSU recruitment?
faq.answer: A: Yes, after online submission, hard copy with documents must be sent by post

faq.question: Q5. Can candidates apply for multiple posts in CSU?
faq.answer: A: Yes, separate application for each post, but avoid same Schedule posts.

faq.question: Q6. How to apply for Central Sanskrit University Non-Teaching posts?
faq.answer: A: Online via SAMARTH portal + send hard copy to New Delhi address.

faq.question: Q7. Are there reserved posts for PwBD and Ex-Servicemen in CSU?
faq.answer: A: Yes, multiple posts reserved for PwBD (categories a-e) and ESM.

faq.question: Q8. What is the mode of recruitment for most posts?
faq.answer: A: Direct recruitment (some posts also by deputation).
